content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 16, 1992

Eduardo A. Rivera
City Attorney
City of Calexico
408 Heber Avenue
Calexico, CA 92231

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-92-595

Dear Mr. Rivera:

This is in response to your letter requesting assistance as
Calexico City Attorney with respect to the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ Since you have not named
the specific official on whose behalf you have requested this
advice, we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance.²

QUESTION

Is a redevelopment agency member, who is a practicing
physician at a local community hospital in her private capacity,
prohibited from participating in the hiring of a new executive
director for the city redevelopment agency, where the nominee for
the position is the hospital administrator at the board member's
hospital?

___________________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section
83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95812 ● (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-92-595
Page 2


CONCLUSION

Absent some economic interest in the hospital or nominee as
set forth in Section 87103, or some direct financial effect on the
board member personally resulting from the decision, the board
member may participate in decisions concerning the new executive
director.

FACTS

According to your facts, a city redevelopment agency member
is the chief of staff, in her private capacity, at a community
hospital in the jurisdiction. You stated that the community
hospital is a hospital district and a public entity.

The redevelopment agency is currently seeking a new executive
director for the redevelopment agency. The hospital administrator
of the community hospital at which the board member is employed is
currently under consideration for the redevelopment agency
position. You stated that the nominee earns an annual salary from
the hospital district of $65,000 plus fringe benefits and that the
gross annual receipts of the community hospital are more than
$1,000,000 and less than $10,000,000.

ANALYSIS

Economic Interests

Section 87100 of the Act prohibits any public official³ from
making, participating in making, or otherwise using his or her
official position to influence a governmental decision in which
the official has a financial interest.

Section 87103 specifies that a public official has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the
official or a member of his or her immediate family or on:

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty

³ A "public official" is defined in Section 82048 and Regulation
18700 as every natural person who is a member, officer, employee,
or consultant of a state or local government agency. This
definition would include a member of the Board of Directors of the
Calexico Redevelopment Agency.

Our File No. I-92-595
Page 3


dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

(d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

Section 87103(c) and (d).

According to your facts, the board member may have a conflict
of interest in the decision if the board member has an economic
interest in (1) the nominee, who is directly involved in the
decision, or, (2) the hospital, which is indirectly involved in
the decision.

A. The Hospital District

Generally, a public official is considered to have an
economic interest in his or her employer and any person or
business entity that has been a source of income to the official.
(Section 87103(c) and (d).) In your letter you stated that the
board member is employed with a local community hospital in the
jurisdiction as chief of staff. Presumably, the board member has
received more than $250 from the hospital in the past 12 months.

However, you stated that the community hospital is a hospital
district, and therefore a public entity under the Act. (Sheeks
Advice Letter, No. A-90-026.) Salary and reimbursement for
expenses or per diem received from a state, local, or federal
government agency is expressly exempted from the definition of
"income" for purposes of the Act. (Section 82030(b)(2).) Thus,
so long as the payments the board member receives from the
hospital are considered salary from the public entity, this would
not create a conflict of interest with respect to decisions that
indirectly affect the hospital. (Section 87103(c); Jenke Advice
Letter, No. A-83-001.)

Moreover, local government agencies are not organizations or
enterprises operated for profit, and therefore are not business
entities under the Act. Thus, the board member's status as an
employee of the community hospital will not create a conflict of
interest concerning decisions affecting the hospital or hospital
district. (Section 87103(d); Section 82005; Darcy Advice Letter,
No. I-87-296.)

B. The Nominee

You have not provided any facts indicating that the nominee
is an economic interest of the board member, such as a source of
income or gifts. Absent some other disqualifying financial

Our File No. I-92-595
Page 4


interest in the decision as set forth in Section 87103 or some
direct financial effect on the board member personally resulting
from the decision, the board member may participate in decisions
concerning the new executive director. (Sampson Advise Letter,
No. I-89-196.)

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
     Counsel, Legal Division
